com.launchdarkly.sdk

Class LDValueConverter

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.launchdarkly.sdk.LDValueConverter

  • public final class LDValueConverter
extends java.lang.Object
    Converts an LDValue tree into a tree of plain Java values (String, Long, Double, Boolean, List, Map, or null).

    Conversion is defensive: it never throws on malformed or pathological input. Numbers are decoded to Long when they are mathematically integral and within the IEEE-754 exact-integer range (|value| <= 2^53); otherwise they are decoded to Double. Whole numbers outside ±2^53 cannot be represented exactly and are returned as the nearest Double. Conversion depth is capped (see MAX_DEPTH); values nested more deeply than the cap are dropped (rendered as null) to bound stack usage on adversarial input.

    Object fields are stored in a LinkedHashMap to preserve insertion order, and all returned collections are unmodifiable.

    • Field Summary

      Fields 
      Modifier and Type Field and Description
      static int MAX_DEPTH
      Maximum nesting depth converted before deeper values are dropped.

    • Method Detail

      • toJavaObject

        public static java.lang.Object toJavaObject(LDValue value)
        Converts an LDValue to a plain Java value.
        Parameters:
        value - the value to convert; may be null
        Returns:
        the converted value, or null if the input is null or JSON null

      • toMap

        public static java.util.Map<java.lang.String,java.lang.Object> toMap(LDValue value)
        Converts an LDValue object to an unmodifiable Map<String, Object>.
        Parameters:
        value - the value to convert
        Returns:
        the converted map; null if value is not a JSON object
